rationale: Title X is stronger overall because it preserves the key exposure, outcome, procedure, population, imaging method, and sample size. The MRI-based cohort of 353 patients gives useful specificity for JICE readers and makes the title feel more concrete. Title Y is journal-appropriate and clear, but it is less informative because it omits MRI and sample size, while the repeated full terms make it longer and flatter.
loser_weakness: Title Y is more generic and less specific, with reduced appeal from repetition and omission of the imaging basis and cohort size.
